title: Advanced Excel Toolkit
subhead: Master the tools real accountants use to tame messy data.

chunks.title: Welcome to the Data Dungeon
chunks.bodyHtml:
In school, you get clean T-accounts. In the real world, you get a 5,000-row CSV export from a broken expense system.

As a Staff Accountant, your job is to turn raw, messy data into clean Journal Entries. To do this, you need three tools: Data Cleaning, VLOOKUP/XLOOKUP, and PivotTables.

chunks.title: Lab: The Messy Expense Export
chunks.instructionsHtml:
Your Task:

  1. Download the raw expense data below.
  2. Use Text-to-Columns to split the messy Date column.
  3. Use VLOOKUP to assign a GL Account Number to each expense category.
  4. Create a PivotTable summarizing total expenses by GL Account.
chunks.reflectionPrompt: What was the total for Travel Expenses (GL 6400)?

questions.q: Why is XLOOKUP generally preferred over VLOOKUP in modern accounting?
options: It defaults to an exact match and can look left
questions.explanation: XLOOKUP defaults to exact match (no more adding 'FALSE' at the end) and can return arrays to the left of the lookup value.
